title: Censored time-to-next-pit model instead of pure binary classification
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem: many rows are just earlier snapshots of the same eventual stop, and final stints are right-censored.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem: many rows are just earlier snapshots of the same eventual stop, and final stints are right-censored. Survival/discrete-hazard formulations use that structure directly instead of forcing every row into an isolated binary label.
implementation_hint: Create `laps_to_next_pit` within each `Race_Year x Driver` sequence. For rows with no later pit, mark them right-censored at `LapsRemaining_Est`. Train either a discrete-time hazard model or an XGBoost `survival:aft` model, then convert its output into `P(stop on next lap)` or use its predicted horizon as a blend feature into the current AUC model.
expected_effect: Better separation in late-stint edge cases, more efficient use of censored finish data, and a complementary score that should blend well with the current tree baseline.
risk: AFT gives time-to-event structure but not a next-lap hazard for free; the probability conversion must be handled carefully, and bad censoring logic can hurt more than help.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
